{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query is about the initial clicking of TMJ while opening. Document [0] states that clicking is a sound of an uncoordinated movement of the condylar head and the articular disc. This implies that the condyle is not in the correct position relative to the disc during opening, leading to a click. Document [10] explains that during mouth opening, the condyle can move laterally through rotation, followed by anterior sliding of the same condylar structure. This anterior sliding is what causes the click when the disc does not move in sync with the condyle. Therefore, the condyle is protruded in respect to the articular disc during this movement.", "answer_choice": "A"}
